Have you ever just looked somebody in the eyes who you care about or somebody that you feel you may have upset or somebody that may just be catching completely off guard who's looked at you and been like, hey, we need to talk. Check this out. As soon as you hear those words, if you're like me, back before I was comfortable with difficult conversations, I was just getting immediate like, fuck, what do you want to talk to me about? Like, what is this? So when you're in that mentality, you have to first acknowledge the fact that you're in a position where you need this course about how to prop and prosper by being able to have difficult conversations with people. So sometimes in life, whether it be employees, whether it be partners, whether it be bosses, whether it be friends, family, enemies, no matter who it is, or to strangers, sometimes you have to literally look people in the eyes and it goes a little above being direct. It goes into more of being able to have a difficult conversation. It's being able to be the person on the other side and say, I think we need to have a talk with them. It's being able to frame those kinds of conversations to have a positive outcome and not have both parties feel charged and stressed afterwards. It's being able to have clarity in the difficult conversation. And so I wanted to give you a few points, if you will, on what to do and kind of a strategy on how to approach a difficult conversation. So without further ado, difficult con bows. So check this out. So in difficult conversations, the way you want to start is in the approach. It's taking a moment to say, how do I want to approach this? You know, I had my grandfather one time, he gave me a hit man example. He said, no Jeremy, sometimes you need to approach people in life like you're a hit man and you're going in and you have three points, you have three facts that are so direct and so to the point that it's like hit man, you know, hit man will come in and he'll shoot one, two, and one in the heart or sometimes one in the head, two in the heart depending on how you look at it, which hit man you see. Hit man, once again, so I'm trying to shoot three direct, just move right past the ego, the purest form of truth is getting past the barriers that the person may develop. Like I'm penetrating with that hit man shot, right? That's in my approach sometimes in a difficult conversation. Other times, a little softer. Hey, we need to have a talk. And that's how I say it. I don't sit down and I'm like, hey, we need to have a talk. You know, I don't give that anxiety. I don't approach the conversation with the emotional state. I don't want the other person to be in. I approach it in the state that I want the other person to be in as I'm in the conversation. And that's sometimes a good mentality to have. But let's just also have one more approach. Sometimes I do want the person to have a certain mentality while I'm in the difficult conversation. I do want them to have a certain kind of emotion, but still I don't then want to approach them in a certain kind of state to be in their state. I want to approach them in a different mentality. So three kinds of approach. The actual delivery matters a lot. So I've learned this time and time again. I've learned it from staff members. I've learned it from girlfriends. I've learned it from friends where sometimes my delivery, I come off like an asshole. Sometimes I come off like a dick, guys. And that being said, your delivery matters. So I've had to learn in life the hard way, like everything else that I'm teaching you in profit and prosper. Your delivery can sometimes fuck up a difficult conversation. Sometimes in your approach, you know, after you've positioned it well enough, then you just come in and you just ruin the whole thing. They don't hear you. You didn't have the hitman approach. You didn't hit them with the three pronged, bull points of what they needed to hear and what you needed to say and what could get them to actually hear it. You failed in the delivery. You were too aggressive. You were too direct. You were too one-sided. You were too transparent with ill intention. Whatever it is your delivery was off. Delivery is very important to a difficult conversation. But to be in a position where you constantly remember the other person's statement, their perspective, how are they going to best perceive what you're talking about? How are they going to actually learn and acknowledge that you're right or that whatever point you're trying to get across is true to them? How do they accept that truth? How is your delivery? Now, check this out. My opinion, closing the cycle is the third most important point to a difficult conversation because at the point of the close, it's commonly like two ways you can kind of walk up and have this split in the road. It's either very negative or it's very positive. At the close, which is going to be right here at this fork in the road, you have positive and you have negative. This represents the relationship itself. Your relationship with this individual reaches a point through a difficult conversation. As you transcend during the difficult conversation, you have your approach. You manage to get past that barrier. You have your delivery. Then right here, you have your close. At your close, once again, you can go towards the positive or you can go towards the negative. You're really up, once again, down your end of the cycle. You're in a position where you're not one-sided and you're thinking about parties, you're taking about perspectives, you're ending the deal to where there's a mutual value exchange or whatever it is that you're trying to get your point across with, whether it be a break up, whether it be whatever is going on in your life that you need to address, whether it be a business partner that's fucking you over on money, whoever it is in life. We need you to have a difficult conversation about approaching the person and being direct, thinking of how you want them to be in that emotional state. Maybe you want to approach it empty. Maybe you want to approach it with a certain kind of bait that's going to put them in a certain state. Maybe you want to approach it and say, hey, let's have a talk. Maybe you want to hit me with a hitman approach. You're delivering itself very critical. That's how they're going to interpret it. That's the middle section here. Some people don't even get to the close and their relationship just gets cut off right here. The point just gets blocked and then you come back and just continuously hit this wall because you just can't deliver time and time and time and getting stuck here in this endless cycle. You just get vicious right here if you can't get past the delivery lines. Once you get to the point where you close, once again, it's either positive or it's negative. It's all based on the fact that if you get the other person to value, if you've overcome that fairness factor, and if you're in agreement, the number one thing about this entire motto is that you want the other person to be in agreement and be accepting of what it is that you're trying to get across in a difficult approach by taking in and considering what their perspective is going to be on how they are taking the information. Anyway, with this, make sure you have difficult conversations in life because sometimes on your path to profit and prospering, you're going to hit a snag it to. We have some serious talks.
